title: Garlic Naan Tandoor Bread

seo.title: Garlic Naan Tandoor Bread Recipe: Easy & Authentic Indian
seo.metaDescription: Learn to make authentic Garlic Naan Tandoor Bread at home! This easy Indian recipe yields soft, fluffy bread, perfect for any meal. Get step-by-step instructions.

seo.h1: Authentic Garlic Naan Tandoor Bread Recipe

seo.imageAlt: Freshly baked homemade Garlic Naan Tandoor Bread, soft and fluffy, garnished with cilantro.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy warm, soft, and garlicky homemade Garlic Naan Tandoor Bread, perfect for dipping in your favorite curry!

description: Garlic Naan Tandoor Bread is a beloved Indian flatbread, known for its soft, chewy texture and irresistible garlic flavor. Traditionally cooked in a tandoor, this recipe guides you to achieve that authentic taste at home, perfect for scooping up rich curries. Each bite offers a delightful blend of subtle sweetness and pungent garlic, making it a star on any Indian dining table.

instructions.title: Activate the Yeast
instructions.text: In a small bowl, combine the warm milk, granulated sugar, and active dry yeast. Stir gently and let it sit for 5-10 minutes until a foamy layer appears on top, indicating the yeast is active and ready.

instructions.title: Prepare the Dough Base
instructions.text: In a large mixing bowl, combine the all-purpose flour and salt. Add the activated yeast mixture, plain yogurt, and 1 tablespoon of the melted ghee or oil. Mix these ingredients thoroughly with a spoon or your hands until a shaggy dough forms.

instructions.title: Knead the Dough
instructions.text: Transfer the dough to a lightly floured surface. Knead the dough for 8-10 minutes continuously until it becomes smooth, soft, and elastic. If the dough feels too sticky, add a tiny bit more flour; if it's too dry, add a few drops of water.

instructions.title: First Rise of the Dough
instructions.text: Lightly grease a clean large bowl with a little oil. Place the kneaded dough in the bowl, turning it once to coat all sides with oil. Cover the bowl tightly with a clean kitchen towel or plastic wrap and let it rise in a warm place for 1.5 to 2 hours, or until the dough has doubled in size.

instructions.title: Portion and Prepare Topping
instructions.text: While the dough is rising, finely mince the garlic cloves and chop the fresh cilantro. Once the dough has doubled, gently punch it down to release the air. Divide the dough into 6-8 equal portions and roll each portion into a smooth ball.

instructions.title: Shape and Top the Naan
instructions.text: On a lightly floured surface, take one dough ball and roll it out using a rolling pin into an oval or teardrop shape, approximately 6-8 inches long and 1/4 inch thick. Press a small amount of the minced garlic and chopped cilantro onto one side of the naan dough.

instructions.title: Cook the Naan (Stovetop Method)
instructions.text: Heat a cast iron skillet or a heavy-bottomed non-stick pan over high heat until it is smoking hot. Place one naan, garlic-side up, onto the hot skillet. Cook for 1-2 minutes until you see bubbles appearing on the surface. Flip the naan and cook the other side for another 1-2 minutes until golden brown spots appear.

instructions.title: Brush and Serve Warm
instructions.text: Once cooked, immediately transfer the hot naan to a plate and generously brush it with the remaining melted ghee or butter. Repeat the cooking process with the remaining dough portions. Serve the warm, fluffy Garlic Naan Tandoor Bread immediately with your favorite Indian curries, dals, or gravies.

tips: For truly authentic flavor and texture, ensure your pan is extremely hot before placing the naan. This mimics the high heat of a tandoor, creating those characteristic bubbles and slightly charred spots.

If you don't have fresh garlic, garlic powder can be used, but fresh minced garlic offers a superior aroma and flavor. For extra richness and a traditional taste, always opt for ghee instead of butter for brushing.

Naan is best served fresh and warm straight off the pan. If you happen to have leftovers, lightly sprinkle them with water, wrap in foil, and reheat in a warm oven or microwave for a few seconds to restore their softness.
article: Garlic Naan Tandoor Bread is a beloved Indian Baking dish enjoyed across Indian. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
